title: Bioinformatics Scientists
description: Conduct research using bioinformatics theory and methods in areas such as pharmaceuticals, medical technology, biotechnology, computational biology, proteomics, computer information science, biology and medical informatics. May design databases and develop algorithms for processing and analyzing genomic information, or other biological information.

tasks.text: Develop new software applications or customize existing applications to meet specific scientific project needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Communicate research results through conference presentations, scientific publications, or project reports.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create novel computational approaches and analytical tools as required by research goals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult with researchers to analyze problems, recommend technology-based solutions, or determine computational strategies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze large molecular datasets, such as raw microarray data, genomic sequence data, or proteomics data, for clinical or basic research purposes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of new biochemistries, instrumentation, or software by reading scientific literature and attending professional conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop data models and databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile data for use in activities, such as gene expression profiling, genome annotation, or structural bioinformatics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design and apply bioinformatics algorithms including unsupervised and supervised machine learning, dynamic programming, or graphic algorithms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Manipulate publicly accessible, commercial, or proprietary genomic, proteomic, or post-genomic databases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct the work of technicians and information technology staff applying bioinformatics tools or applications in areas such as proteomics, transcriptomics, metabolomics, or clinical bioinformatics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide statistical and computational tools for biologically based activities, such as genetic analysis, measurement of gene expression, or gene function determination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create or modify web-based bioinformatics tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Improve user interfaces to bioinformatics software and databases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with departments, such as marketing, business development, or operations, to coordinate product development or improvement.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend new systems and processes to improve operations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct others in the selection and use of bioinformatics tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collaborate with software developers in the development and modification of commercial bioinformatics software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test new and updated bioinformatics tools and software.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare summary statistics of information regarding human genomes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
